results.title: TailMux: Multiple Tailscale tailnets at once, no switching + ...
results.content:

TailMux

# TailMux

Launching today

## Multiple Tailscale tailnets at once, no switching + no VM

4 followers

## Multiple Tailscale tailnets at once, no switching + no VM

4 followers

Visit website

VPN client

The official Tailscale client keeps one tailnet active at a time. TailMux makes work and personal tailnets reachable simultaneously on macOS and Linux, without switching accounts, running multiple system daemons, or using a VM. It runs an isolated embedded node per profile and routes by hostname, with strict no-fallback isolation. Use SSH, RDP/SMB, browsers, curl, git, and npm across tailnets at the same time.

 Overview
 Reviews
 Alternatives
 Team
 More

Payment Required

Launch tags:Mac•Developer Tools•Security

Launch Team

Subscribe

Blocks.ai [...] Hey Product Hunt 👋 I'm the maker. TailMux started as a personal annoyance: I run a work tailnet and a personal one, and the official client can only be active on one at a time. Fast user switching means constantly running tailscale switch and dropping the other connection. The workarounds (two daemons, userspace SOCKS5, a whole VM) all felt clunky on macOS, so I built something that runs an isolated embedded Tailscale node per tailnet and routes traffic by hostname suffix. Both tailnets are live at the same time, with strict isolation so nothing leaks between them. It covers SSH, RDP/SMB tunnels, per-tailnet browser routing, and routing curl/git/npm. It's a one-time $4.99 license (a year of updates, keep your version forever). It's not affiliated with Tailscale — just a companion tool. It [...] not affiliated with Tailscale — just a companion tool. It scratched my own itch and I use it daily; I'd love feedback from anyone juggling multiple tailnets. Ask me anything.

# TailMuxAlternatives [...] Subscribe

# TailMuxAlternatives

Love TailMux for juggling multiple tailnets without switching? Explore alternatives: Tailscale for battle-tested mesh VPNs, Ngrok for secure public tunnels, Netmaker for WireGuard® fleets, plus Pangolin and Passepartout VPN for identity-first and iOS/macOS needs.
